Engineering and Information Technology (KFUEIT) is based on merit and is open for various undergraduate (BS), graduate (MS), and doctoral (PhD) programs. Applicants must meet specific academic requirements and pass the university’s entry test, followed by an interview. The university typically announces admissions for different programs once or twice a year, and interested candidates can apply online through the official KFUEIT website or by visiting the admissions office.

records.source_section_label: Fine Arts and Textile Design Exhibition Showcases Student Creativity at KFUEIT - KFUEIT

record_payload.name: Fine Arts and Textile Design Exhibition Showcases Student Creativity at KFUEIT - KFUEIT
record_payload.content:
Home Happenings At KFUEIT Fine Arts and Textile Design Exhibition Showcases Student Creativity at KFUEIT
Fine Arts and Textile Design Exhibition Showcases Student Creativity at KFUEIT
Happenings At KFUEIT Events General News News & Updates Institute of Humanities & Social Sciences News & Events Art & Project Exhibition
Vice Chancellor Vice Chancellor Prof. Ir. Dr. Amir Azam Khan Khwaja Fareed University of Engineering and Information Technology (KFUEIT) has inaugurated a Fine Arts and Textile Design exhibition, showcasing the final projects of graduating students. The four-day exhibition features 36 final-year projects, including six by Fine Arts students and 26 by Textile Design students.
Among the themes explored in the artwork are Phulkari embroidery, faded childhood memories, Mughal art, camel art, truck art, improvisational jugaad, and acid attacks. These themes captivated visitors and highlighted the students' talent and creativity.
Vice Chancellor Prof. Ir. Dr. Amir Azam Khan toured the displays, interacted with students, and praised their work. He commended the originality and depth of the projects, noting that they reflect students' dedication, innovation, and strong cultural connections. Prof. Dr. Amir Azam Khan emphasized that the university remains committed to supporting and refining students' skills, promoting their work across various platforms to expand their opportunities and career prospects.
Also in attendance were Associate Dean Dr. Ahmed Sohaib, Director of the Institute of Humanities and Arts Dr. Anwar Farooq, faculty members Arshad Rahmani, Muhammad Tayyab, Dr. Majid Rasheed, and a large number of students. The exhibition attracted participants from various departments, who appreciated their peers' efforts and celebrated the diverse talent at KFUEIT.
The event underscores KFUEIT’s commitment to nurturing creativity, connecting students with cultural heritage, and equipping them with the skills to succeed in the modern world.

record_payload.name: KFUEIT Book Fair & Literary Festival 2026 - KFUEIT
record_payload.content:
Home Events KFUEIT Book Fair & Literary Festival 2026
KFUEIT Book Fair & Literary Festival 2026
Immerse yourself in a world of stories, ideas, and imagination at the KFUEIT Book Fair & Literary Festival 2026 . Taking place on May 6–7, 2026 , starting at 11:00 AM , this two-day event transforms the Civil Building into a hub of creativity and intellectual engagement.
Organized by the KFUEIT Central Library in collaboration with the KFUEIT Readers’ & Writers’ Club , the festival offers a unique opportunity to explore a vast collection of books and participate in literary activities designed to inspire. Whether you are a dedicated bibliophile or a casual reader, we invite you to join the campus community in celebrating the power of literature and the enduring impact of the written word.

records.source_section_label: Vision, Mission and Core Values - KFUEIT

record_payload.title: Vision, Mission and Core Values - KFUEIT
record_payload.content:
Home Vision, Mission and Core Values
Vision, Mission and Core Values
To become a World Class University that contributes significantly for the development of regional economy to uplift local community by becoming a power house of intellectuals and human capital generation.
To offer an educational experience wherein: The curriculum and its delivery confirm to international standards The students are provided an environment for wholesome development of their personality and creative potential The graduates produced are most sought after by prospective employers
The curriculum and its delivery confirm to international standards
The students are provided an environment for wholesome development of their personality and creative potential
The graduates produced are most sought after by prospective employers
To conduct research to solve local and national problems requiring knowledge based solutions.
Establish 30 world-class undergraduate degree programs in six domains of Sciences & Mathematics, Engineering, Information & Communication Technology, Life Sciences & Healthcare, Management, Social & Legal Sciences, Humanities & Arts.
Conduct research in inter-disciplinary areas of local and national need to produce intellectual capital (knowledge, solutions, Intellectual Property (IP)) and human capital (PhDs):
Applied & Open Source Information Technology (Education, Health and Agriculture)
Embedded Systems (Next Generation Smartphones, Tablets, Security Cameras, Home Automation Systems, Building Controls Systems, Factory & Production Line Automation for Agri-based Industries, Electric Vehicles Control Systems)
Biotechnology (better crops and fruit varieties)
Water Resource Engineering / Management
Environmental Science / Engineering
Renewable Energy (Biomass, Solar PV, Wind and others)
Information Security (Information Assurance, Cyber Warfare, Digital Forensics)
Materials Engineering (Solid Electrolytes, Organic Photovoltaics, Nano-materials)
Power Storage, Conversion & Management
Engineering Management, Healthcare Management and Supply Chain Management
At Fareed University of Engineering & Information Technology, Rahim Yar Khan we have chosen a set of core values which fully embody our vision and mission. These core values permeate all our activities and form a benchmark to judge our efforts against. These core values are the qualities that we want to maximize in our students, faculty and staff. Furthermore, we believe that these core values will allow us to transform our society into an advance one. Our core values are based on our acronym so that they become easy for us to remember.
A look at the shades of meaning conveyed by these core values will help us understand fully the impactthat we intend these core values to have on ourstudents, faculty and staff:

KFUEIT Free Medical Camp: Committed to a Healthier Community
Khwaja Fareed University of Engineering & Information Technology (KFUEIT) is proud to announce a Free Medical Camp on April 29, 2026 , bringing essential healthcare services directly to your doorstep. This initiative aims to foster a healthier campus and community by providing expert medical consultations, basic diagnostic tests, and comprehensive health screenings at absolutely no cost. We invite all students, faculty, and community members to take advantage of this valuable opportunity to prioritize their well-being and benefit from professional medical guidance in a supportive and accessible environment.

Friendly Badminton Championship
The Directorate of Sports and Hostel Department at Khwaja Fareed University of Engineering and Information Technology (KFUEIT) successfully organized a friendly badminton championship, bringing together Day Scholars and hostel students.
The event aimed to promote physical activity while fostering teamwork and camaraderie among participants. Held at the university’s sports complex, the championship created a lively atmosphere, offering an enjoyable experience for both players and spectators.
Such events play a vital role in promoting sportsmanship, well-being, and a sense of belonging within the university community, further strengthening the spirit of unity at KFUEIT.

records.source_section_label: Graduates Welcomed by TANG International Group China - KFUEIT

record_payload.title: Graduates Welcomed by TANG International Group China - KFUEIT
record_payload.content:
Home Campus Life Graduates Welcomed by TANG International Group China
Graduates Welcomed by TANG International Group China
KFUEIT Graduates Welcomed by TANG International Group, China
A grand reception ceremony was held in Jinzhou, China, to honor the graduates of Khwaja Fareed University of Engineering and Information Technology (KFUEIT). Dignitaries from Tang International Group graced the occasion and warmly welcomed the fifty-member delegation from KFUEIT.
KFUEIT Vice-Chancellor delivered a special video message, extending congratulations to the students for their opportunity to pursue education and employment prospects in China. He highlighted the significant achievement of KFUEIT students in securing training and employment opportunities in China, emphasizing their potential contribution to the national economy through global platforms. Dr. Murtaza underlined that such initiatives underscore the robust friendship between Pakistan and China.
Officials from Tang International Group also expressed their best wishes, and tokens of appreciation were exchanged during the event.
This collaboration symbolizes the strong ties between Pakistan and China and demonstrates the global recognition of KFUEIT's graduates.

records.source_section_label: Huge Congratulations to the winner of “Entrepreneurial Female Youth Pitch Competition” - KFUEIT

record_payload.name: Huge Congratulations to the winner of “Entrepreneurial Female Youth Pitch Competition” - KFUEIT
record_payload.content:
Home General News Huge Congratulations to the winner of “Entrepreneurial Female Youth Pitch Competition”
Huge Congratulations to the winner of “Entrepreneurial Female Youth Pitch Competition”
KFUEIT is proud to recognize the outstanding achievement of Ms. Faiza Amir Khakwani, a MBA 4th Semester student, who recently secured the grant of 1 million by winning Entrepreneurial Female Youth Pitch Competition, organized by Women Development Department, Gov of Punjab.
The successful pitch of a startup is a significant undertaking, and Ms. Faiza Amir Khakwani demonstrated exceptional strategic thinking and presentation skills. This victory highlights the talent and drive fostered within KFUEIT, Rahim Yar Khan.
The university also commends the efforts of Dr. Sidra Ghazanfar, Focal Person of “Entrepreneurial Female Youth Pitch Competition”, for her dedicated mentorship and guidance.
The “Entrepreneurial Female Youth Pitch Competition” was initiated by Women Development Department, Gov of Punjab to encourage female university students across Punjab to transform their innovative ideas into sustainable businesses. By providing a structured pathway ranging from idea pitching and mentorship to seed funding and follow-up support the program directly aligns with WDD’s mission to empower women as drivers of economic growth, job creation, and community development.

records.source_section_label: Transport - KFUEIT

record_payload.title: Transport - KFUEIT
record_payload.content: The transport department renders the quality policy of KFUEIT by providing an unperturbed and cost-effective transport facility to students, faculty and staff. University has its own fleet of buses that includes 20 HINO/ISUZU Buses, one Toyota Coaster, one Toyota Hi-Ace, one Ambulance and other pool vehicles. Pickup and drop-off services are available for locations within the city and its surrounding areas within a 100 km radius. Moreover, transport is also provided to students for outdoor activities, sightseeing, picnic, excursion, study and field tour etc.

record_payload.content:
Home Campus Life Project Exhibition Showcases
Campus Life Art & Project Exhibition
Final Year Project Exhibition Showcases Innovation at KFUEIT
The Institute of Mechanical and Manufacturing Engineering at Khwaja Fareed University of Engineering and Information Technology (KFUEIT) recently hosted its eagerly awaited Final Year Project Exhibition, spotlighting the culmination of students' hard work and innovation.
The distinguished Vice Chancellor, Prof. Dr. Shahzad Murtaza, inaugurated the exhibition. Throughout the event, he attentively observed the diverse array of projects presented by the students, praising their efforts, creativity, and dedication. The Vice Chancellor also extended his appreciation to the organizers for their meticulous planning and execution.
The exhibition provided a platform for students to showcase their technical prowess, problem-solving abilities, and innovative ideas. It offered an invaluable opportunity for industry professionals, faculty members, and guests to witness the ingenuity and potential of the future engineers graduating from KFUEIT.
The Institute of Mechanical and Manufacturing Engineering remains steadfast in its commitment to nurturing a culture of innovation and excellence, empowering students to emerge as leaders in their respective fields. Alongside Prof. Dr. Shahzad Murtaza, esteemed faculty members including Dr. Shahid Attique, Dr. Naeem Ullah Anmool, Dr. Muhammad Umar, and Dr. Anwar Farooq graced the occasion with their presence.

records.source_section_label: Why KFUEIT - KFUEIT

record_payload.name: Why KFUEIT - KFUEIT
record_payload.content:
A Premium Institution in Making
The KFUEIT Rahim Yar Khan is the first university in a radius of 200 km embarking upon its plans to spread quality education in a highly professional environment with grace, pride and dignity. This institution is nothing lesser than a blessing for the area of Rahim Khan in particular and Southern Punjab and Northern Sindh in general. Looking at its aggressive expansion plans w.r.t. infrastructural development, hiring talented teaching and non-teaching staff, adding new programs in academic portfolio, building systems & policies and ensuring professional environment are evident of the fact that “A Premium Institution is in Making”.
We promote academic excellence through professional environment at KFUEIT where students are treated with respect and care while having certain level of freedom. Our culture and employees are catalyst in ensuring an environment where students are provided with numerous opportunities to learn, grow and at the same time make their stay at this university enjoyable and life time experience.
State of Art Infrastructure & Facilities
The current area of KFUEIT in Phase-1 is 279 acres and with acquisition of 200 acres more land in Phase-2 the total area sums up to 479 acres. The substantial infrastructural development of first phase has given university a great shape and with the completion of phase 2, this institution will stand out amongst top institutes of the country. The development, facilities and systems being installed here are state of the art, while laying foundation with futuristic approach.
Hi-tech ICT System, automation and digitalization
Well-equipped and modernized security set up
Automated and computerized admission, testing and hiring processes
Departmental buildings and class rooms equipped with latest technologies
Spacious auditorium and Library
Providing wide range of sports infrastructure and facilities
Hostel facility for boys and girls
Environmental friendly campus with dedicated horticulture department
Development of Specialized and Transferrable Skills
Education at KFUEIT allows you to get exposed to various technical subjects and skillset where you will be able to develop desired aspects of your traits and polish soft skills along with academic laurels. Students learn here the skills that are transferable to most industries such as communications, decision making, problem solving, project management, innovation and teamwork. The students of KFUEIT will have the knowledge to contribute to a rapidly changing technological environment.
Personal Development Opportunities
During their course of studies, our students come across with numerous opportunities within and outside the university to learn and develop what it takes to become a better human being and successful professional. Many curricular and extracurricular activities are arranged by the university whereas many more are initiated and arranged by students themselves. These project and activities give multidimensional platforms to students in improving their soft skills, build more networking among university colleagues and industry stakeholders.
In order to make the students prepared for upcoming challenges and requirements of the practical world, KFUEIT organizes regular educational trips and internships. Our Career Counselling and Industrial Placement Office plays an instrumental role in shaping up student’s future plans and educational aspirations by inviting guest speakers from relevant industries, arranging industrial visits, conducting mock interviews, recommendations for internships and job placements.
The university provides a perfect blend of academic and extracurricular activities, because we believe in grooming our students by getting them exposed to number of real World scenarios. The events and activities like sports week, body building competition, women pink day, declamation competitions, Mushaira / bait baazi, fun fairs, cultural days, welcome & farewell parties are adding colors to the campus life. Our students have won many laurels for the university.

record_payload.overview_text:
Home Campus Life Inter-Departmental Sports Competitions
Inter-Departmental Sports Competitions
Campus Life Department of Agricultural Engineering News & Events Sports & Fitness
KFUEIT Hosts Exciting Inter-Departmental Sports Competitions
The Directorate of Sports at Khwaja Fareed University of Engineering and Information Technology (KFUEIT) organized thrilling cricket matches and Tug of War competitions, bringing together students for a series of energetic events. Both male and female students from the Departments of Physical Education and Humanities participated in cricket matches, while the Departments of Agriculture and Physical Education competed in Tug of War contests, with separate events for male and female students.
These activities created a vibrant and positive atmosphere on campus, encouraging teamwork, sportsmanship, and a strong sense of community among participants. The use of the university's scenic circle ground added to the excitement, making the experience memorable for everyone involved.
Initiatives like these play a vital role in promoting physical activity, camaraderie, and student engagement, further enhancing the university's commitment to holistic student development.

2nd Industrial Open House, Career Fair & Project Expo 2026 at KFUEIT
The Directorate of Career Counselling and Industrial Placement at Khwaja Fareed University of Engineering & Information Technology (KFUEIT), in collaboration with ORIC and the Alumni Association, is honored to invite your esteemed organization to the 2nd Industrial Open House, Career Fair & Project Expo 2026 . This flagship event, scheduled for May 12–13, 2026 , serves as a premier bridge between academia and industry, fostering a dynamic environment for recruitment, innovation, and professional networking.
Attendees will have the opportunity to engage with high-caliber talent through Final Year Design Project exhibitions , explore recruitment prospects at the Career Fair , and participate in panel discussions featuring distinguished C-suite executives and industry leaders. We invite you to set up a dedicated recruitment or awareness stall to directly interact with our skilled student body and showcase your organization’s vision.
Venue: KFUEIT Campus, Rahim Yar Khan
Contact for Registration: yasir.niaz@kfueit.edu.pk | +92 335 3420890
We look forward to your participation in strengthening academia–industry linkages and shaping the future of Pakistan's industrial landscape.

records.source_section_label: Art and Design Exhibition - KFUEIT

record_payload.name: Art and Design Exhibition - KFUEIT
record_payload.content:
Home Campus Life Art and Design Exhibition
Campus Life Art & Project Exhibition
Today, Khwaja Fareed University of Engineering and Information Technology witnessed a vibrant showcase of talent at its Art and Design Exhibition. Vice Chancellor, Professor Dr. Shahzad Murtaza, inaugurated the event, joined by distinguished guests including Dr. Ahmed Sohaib and Dr. Muhammad Anwar Farooq from the Institute of Humanities and Arts.
From intricate paintings to innovative textile designs, students from various semesters displayed nearly 300 captivating art pieces. The exhibition, a testament to the university's commitment to holistic education, featured a diverse range of creations including calligraphy, landscaping, and Islamic product design, reflecting both traditional heritage and modern sensibilities.
Speaking to the media, Professor Dr. Murtaza underscored the university's mission to nurture talent and instill practical skills alongside academic knowledge. He emphasized the importance of such platforms in honing students' abilities to meet the evolving needs of society.
Applauding the efforts of the departments and organizers, especially the Institute of Humanities and Arts, Professor Dr. Murtaza reiterated the significance of regular exhibitions in aligning with the university's vision and providing students with valuable opportunities for growth.
The event, attended by Course Coordinators and a multitude of students, not only celebrated creativity but also fostered a spirit of collaboration and appreciation among the university community.

Happenings At KFUEIT General News News & Updates Seminars & Conferences
One Day Artificial Intelligence and Industrial Advancement Exhibition and Seminar on November 7, 2024
The Higher Education Department South Punjab proudly organized the one-day Artificial Intelligence and Industrial Advancement Exhibition and Seminar on November 7, 2024, at Multan Marquee. This landmark event brought together over twenty leading universities and educational institutions, making it a groundbreaking platform for research and innovation under the renowned Triple Helix Model. This model, which fosters collaboration between academia, industry, and government, set the stage for Pakistan’s first-ever AI and industrial innovation expo of this scale.
The expo was officially inaugurated by Additional Chief Secretary Fuad Hashim Rabbani, joined by professors, industry leaders, South Punjab Special Secretaries, and numerous sponsors who united to witness this unique fusion of knowledge, technology, and industrial solutions.
On the direction of our esteemed Vice Chancellor, the KFUEIT Team led by Dr. Muhammad Bilal Tahir, Provost KFUEIT, enthusiastically participated in this event, showcasing the university’s commitment to pioneering research and sustainable solutions. Our delegation included prominent faculty members such as Dr. Badar Ul Islam, Dr. Rana Mujahid, Dr. Adnan Khalil, dedicated staff, and enthusiastic students. They represented KFUEIT with pride, participating in various discussions, presentations, and networking sessions.
A total of 26 innovative projects from fields like Artificial Intelligence, Biotechnology, Green Energy, Material Science, Health, and Environmental Sustainability were presented by our talented undergraduate students. These projects received high praise for their forward-thinking approaches and practical applications. Among them, the project “Resilient Recyclables: Transforming Plastic Waste into Campus Benches” garnered significant attention for its creative approach to sustainability and was a standout in both the exhibition and seminar. The interest in commercializing this project nationwide underscores its potential impact on environmental innovation and practical applications across Pakistan.
The event also featured thought-provoking seminars and live demonstrations, where participants delved into the significance of these projects and explored commercialization pathways. Investors and funders engaged deeply with these projects, expressing keen interest in their development and implementation, recognizing the transformative potential of these scientific innovations.
In a memorable closing ceremony, Additional Chief Secretary South Punjab honored our Vice Chancellor with a shield and presented certificates to KFUEIT’s remarkable students, celebrating their contributions to this inspiring event.
This expo marks an important milestone in Pakistan's journey toward technological and industrial advancement, and KFUEIT is proud to be at the forefront of this movement. Together, we are driving innovation, sustainability, and impactful solutions for a brighter future!

Home General News Colorful Sports Gala Commences at KFUEIT with Enthusiastic Performances
Colorful Sports Gala Commences at KFUEIT with Enthusiastic Performances
General News News & Updates Sports & Fitness
The 8th Annual Sports Gala at Khwaja Fareed University of Engineering and Information Technology (KFUEIT) has officially begun, showcasing vibrant and energetic performances from students. The grand opening ceremony was inaugurated by Vice Chancellor Prof. Ir. Dr. Amir Azam Khan, alongside Pro-Vice Chancellor Prof. Dr. Shahzad Murtaza, Registrar Dr. Muhammad Javed Iqbal, and President of the District Press Club Qaisar Ghafoor. The event witnessed a warm welcome as players and organizers showered flowers upon the esteemed guests.
On the first day, students from various departments participated in a march-past, saluting the stage where dignitaries were present. In his address, the Vice Chancellor emphasized the university's commitment to promoting healthy activities alongside academics. He noted that students' enthusiasm in both academics and sports is highly commendable and reflects positively on their holistic development. He highlighted that sports play a crucial role in keeping youth physically fit and socially productive, shielding them from negative influences.
President Qaisar Ghafoor applauded KFUEIT for its remarkable achievements in a short span, praising the leadership of Prof. Amir Azam Khan and his team. He expressed admiration for the university's academic vibrancy, lush green campus, and well-maintained sports grounds.
The first day featured thrilling competitions in tug-of-war, taekwondo, athletics, cricket, football, volleyball, and handball, with participants demonstrating exceptional talent and sportsmanship. The event was attended by prominent faculty members, including Dr. Muhammad Sagir, Dr. Basharat Gul, Dr. Adnan Bajwa, Dr. Bilal Tahir, and many others, along with a large number of students.
KFUEIT remains steadfast in its mission to foster a balanced environment where education and extracurricular activities go hand in hand.

records.source_section_label: KFUEIT Hosts Annual Sports Gala 2025 with Grand Opening Ceremony - KFUEIT

record_payload.name: KFUEIT Hosts Annual Sports Gala 2025 with Grand Opening Ceremony - KFUEIT
record_payload.content:
Home General News KFUEIT Hosts Annual Sports Gala 2025 with Grand Opening Ceremony
KFUEIT Hosts Annual Sports Gala 2025 with Grand Opening Ceremony
General News News & Updates Sports & Fitness
The Annual Sports Gala 2025 at Khwaja Fareed University of Engineering & Information Technology (KFUEIT) was formally inaugurated in an impressive ceremony held on campus. The event was graced by Deputy Commissioner Rahim Yar Khan, Zahir Anwar Jappa, as the chief guest, while the proceedings were led under the dynamic leadership of the Vice Chancellor, Prof. Ir. Dr. Amir Azam Khan.
A large gathering comprising university deans, faculty members, administrative officers, and a significant number of male and female students attended the opening ceremony, reflecting the vibrant academic and co-curricular culture of the university.
Addressing the participants, the Deputy Commissioner emphasized the importance of sports in promoting positive activities among youth. He highlighted that sports not only contribute to physical fitness but also nurture discipline, teamwork, leadership, and a healthy competitive spirit. He reaffirmed that the Punjab Government and district administration would continue to extend full support for the promotion of co-curricular activities in educational institutions. He also congratulated the university administration on organizing a well-managed Sports Gala and encouraged students to actively participate in sports alongside their academic pursuits.
Speaking on the occasion, Vice Chancellor Prof. Ir. Dr. Amir Azam Khan thanked the Deputy Commissioner for his presence and support. He also expressed gratitude to Mr. Sheikh Imad ud Din, CEO Khaloos Ghee, and Mian Aamir Shahbaz (JPL) for their cooperation and support in promoting sports and youth activities at the university. The Vice Chancellor underlined that KFUEIT accords special importance to sports and co-curricular activities to ensure the holistic development of students.
The Vice Chancellor specially appreciated the efforts of the Sports Department and Director Sports, Dr. Basharat, for their dedicated planning, coordination, and successful organization of the Annual Sports Gala 2025. He acknowledged that the professionalism and commitment of the sports team played a key role in making the event vibrant, disciplined, and engaging for students.
At the conclusion of the ceremony, the Deputy Commissioner visited various sports venues, observed different matches, and appreciated the enthusiasm and performance of the students. The Sports Gala features competitions in cricket, football, volleyball, badminton, athletics, and several other games, promising an exciting and energetic series of events on campus.

record_payload.program_name: Faculty of Humanities and Social Sciences - KFUEIT
record_payload.overview_text:
Faculty of Humanities and Social Sciences
Home Faculties Faculty of Humanities and Social Sciences
Welcome to the Faculty of Humanities and Social Sciences
Dear Students, Faculty Members, and Visitors,
On behalf of the Faculty of Humanities and Social Sciences, I am delighted to welcome you to our vibrant community of scholars. Our faculty is dedicated to fostering intellectual curiosity, creativity and critical thinking. Our mission is to provide a supportive learning environment that encourages students to explore the complexities of human experience, social dynamics and cultural diversity. Through our diverse range of undergraduate and graduate programs, we equip students with the knowledge, skills and values necessary to succeed in an ever-changing world. Our faculty members are renowned researchers and practitioners in their fields, collaborating with international partners to advance knowledge and address global challenges. We offer various opportunities for students to engage in research projects, internships and community service, preparing them for successful careers and responsible citizenship. Explore our web pages to learn more about our programs, research initiatives and events. Join our community to become part of a dynamic and inclusive academic environment.
For inquiries or suggestions, please email asohaib@kfueit.edu.pk
Institute of Humanities and Arts

records.source_section_label: Faculty of Mechanical & Agriculture Engineering - KFUEIT

record_payload.program_name: Faculty of Mechanical & Agriculture Engineering - KFUEIT
record_payload.overview_text:
Faculty of Mechanical & Agriculture Engineering
Home Faculties Faculty of Mechanical & Agriculture Engineering
Email : yasir.niaz@kfueit.edu.pk
I am delighted to welcome you to the Faculty of Mechanical and Agriculture Engineering. There are two departments namely the Department of Agricultural Engineering (AE) and the Institute of Mechanical and Manufacturing Engineering (M&ME). The Department of Agricultural Engineering offers BS, MS, and PhD degree programs in Agricultural Engineering and Agriculture Sciences since 2018 & 19, respectively with remarkable achievements and contributions to the innovation, and well-being of the local society since its inception in 2018. The Institute of Mechanical and Manufacturing Engineering has significantly advanced since its founding in 2014. The department offers BS, MS, and PhD Degree programs in Mechanical Engineering. Our mission is to educate, inspire, and equip the students with the technical expertise and critical thinking skills necessary to address the challenges faced by Pakistan’s agriculture and industrial sector. I am proud to lead a faculty committed to excellence in teaching, research, and innovation. I am pursuing its mission while ensuring a conducive and stimulating environment that fosters creativity, adaptability, and a sense of responsibility towards society. Together, let's harness technology, collaboration, and creative problem-solving to transform the agricultural and industrial sectors.
Department of Agricultural Engineering
Institute of Mechanical & Manufacturing Engineering

record_payload.program_name: Faculty of Biological, Health Sciences & Technology - KFUEIT
record_payload.overview_text:
Faculty of Biological, Health Sciences & Technology
Home Faculties Faculty of Biological, Health Sciences & Technology
Email : jallat.khan@kfueit.edu.pk
As an Associate Dean Faculty of Biological, Health Sciences & Technology (FBHST), I am delighted to welcome and introduce you to the faculty. The FBHST is composed of two institutes: the Institute of Health Sciences and the Institute of Biological Sciences.
The Institute of Biological Sciences, formerly the Department of Biosciences established in 2019, aims to provide cutting-edge research and educational opportunities in the field of Applied Biological Sciences. Elevated to institute status in 2022, it is committed to fostering successful researchers in areas such as Biotechnology, Biochemistry, Forensic Sciences, Bioinformatics, and Microbiology. Our innovative approach equips students with a deep understanding of living systems through advanced molecular, biochemical, biotechnological, and computational methodologies.
Similarly, the Institute of Health Sciences was established in 2019. The institute attained its current status on March 4, 2022, and offers Bachelor’s and Master’s degree programs in Medical Imaging Technology, Medical Laboratory Technology, Optometry, Clinical Psychology, Orthotics & Prosthetics, Aesthetics & Cosmetological Sciences, Doctor of Physical Therapy, and Healthcare Management.

records.source_section_label: KFUEIT Workshop Pushes for Green Technology and Interdisciplinary Solutions to Tackle Industrial Pollution - KFUEIT

record_payload.name: KFUEIT Workshop Pushes for Green Technology and Interdisciplinary Solutions to Tackle Industrial Pollution - KFUEIT
record_payload.content:
Home General News KFUEIT Workshop Pushes for Green Technology and Interdisciplinary Solutions to Tackle Industrial Pollution
KFUEIT Workshop Pushes for Green Technology and Interdisciplinary Solutions to Tackle Industrial Pollution
General News News & Updates Seminars & Conferences
RAHIM YAR KHAN, [Date of Workshop] – Khwaja Fareed University of Engineering and Information Technology (KFUEIT) successfully concluded a consultative workshop focused on the critical relationship between technological innovation and environmental pollution, organized under the prestigious HEC NRPU Project No. 20-14732.
The workshop, titled "Asymmetric impact of technological innovation on environmental pollution in Pakistan using quantile estimation," brought together research scholars from KFUEIT and key representatives from the local industry to discuss urgent environmental challenges and propose policy-level solutions. The event was a collaborative effort by the Office of Research, Innovation and Commercialization (ORIC) KFUEIT, HEC, and the Institute of Business Administration (IBA) KFUEIT.
Dr. Adnan Maqbool, Principal Investigator (PI) of the project, presented his research findings to the participants. Dr. Maqbool highlighted the existence of various technologies capable of mitigating environmental pollution originating from industrial sources. He underscored the necessity of robust policy interventions to minimize the current pollution load, ultimately advocating for a mandatory shift of industrial technology towards green technology adoption across Pakistan.
The workshop also featured a compelling online address from Dr. Muhammad Hafeez of the Institute of Business Management Sciences (IBMS), University of Agriculture. Dr. Hafeez emphasized the vital role of eco-innovation as the primary strategy for managing global environmental challenges. Furthermore, he elaborated on several financial initiatives and incentives being offered by the Government of Pakistan aimed at facilitating the widespread adoption and improvement of green technologies within the industrial landscape.
Summing up the day’s discussions, Engr. Dr. Muhammad Saif Ur Rehman, Director ORIC KFUEIT, provided crucial context. He stressed that environmental pollution is a shared global challenge that cannot be solved by individual entities. He argued that an interdisciplinary approach—combining engineering, policy, and finance—is absolutely necessary to propose valid and workable technological solutions for the industry. Dr. Rehman further highlighted a key constraint: "Any innovative technology, regardless of its efficacy, cannot be scaled up without comprehensive financial integration and rigorous enforcement of environmental regulations."
The consultative workshop concluded with a formal session where the Worthy Vice Chancellor presented souvenirs to the resource persons in recognition of their valuable contributions and distributed certificates to the participating industrial representatives. The event successfully fostered a strong dialogue between academia and industry, setting a clear agenda for future research and policy development in the area of environmental sustainability.

records.source_section_label: Seminar on Policy Engagement for a Legislative Ban on PHOs at KFUEIT - KFUEIT

record_payload.rule_title: Seminar on Policy Engagement for a Legislative Ban on PHOs at KFUEIT - KFUEIT
record_payload.rule_text:
Home General News Seminar on Policy Engagement for a Legislative Ban on PHOs at KFUEIT
Seminar on Policy Engagement for a Legislative Ban on PHOs at KFUEIT
General News News & Updates Seminars & Conferences
The Institute of Food Science & Technology (IFST), KFUEIT successfully hosted an insightful seminar titled “Policy Engagement for a Legislative Ban on Partially Hydrogenated Oils (PHOs)” under the TRANSFORM Pakistan Campaign, in collaboration with Pakistan Youth Change Advocates (PYCA).
The event brought together experts, policymakers, food authorities, public health professionals, youth advocates and IFST students to discuss Pakistan’s path toward eliminating harmful PHOs in line with WHO global guidelines. Participants emphasized the critical role of academia in policy advocacy and promoting a healthier national food environment.
Prof. Dr. Amir Azam Khan, Vice Chancellor KFUEIT (Chief Guest)
Lt Col (R) Shahid Bashir, Principal Danish School (Boys), RYK
Dr. Khalid Mehmood, Principal, Govt. Khawaja Fareed College
Dr. Muhammad Sohail Sharif, Campus Director, IUB
Dr. Sakhawat Ali Randhawa, Public Health Consultant
Mr. M. Asim and Mr. Shakeel, Punjab Food Authority, and SZMCH
Over 50 students and faculty members of IFST actively participated, contributing to a rich and interactive discussion.
Experts unanimously highlighted the urgent need for a legislative ban on PHOs, stronger regulatory frameworks and multi-sector collaboration to safeguard public health.
IFST, KFUEIT remains dedicated to advancing research, public health advocacy and evidence-based policy engagement. This seminar marks a significant step toward eliminating industrially produced trans fats in Pakistan.
Together, let’s work for a healthier, trans-fat–free Pakistan!

record_payload.content:
Home General News KFUEIT Hosts 50th International Seerat Conference
KFUEIT Hosts 50th International Seerat Conference
General News News & Updates Seminars & Conferences
RAHIM YAR KHAN 03-09-2025– In observance of the birth anniversary of Holy Prophet, Hazrat Muhammad (PBUH), Khwaja Fareed University of Engineering & Information Technology (KFUEIT), Rahim Yar Khan, held its 50th International Seerat Conference today at the university's Civil Auditorium.
The conference, which featured distinguished speakers from Pakistan and abroad, was attended in person and online. Vice Chancellor Prof. Ir. Dr. Aamir Azam Khan welcomed the esteemed guests, including Mian Amir Shahbaz, emphasizing the importance of following the Prophet's teachings. "Only by following the Seerat-un-Nabi ﷺ can we transform this world into a peaceful, stable, and prosperous society," he stated. He also highlighted the university's fundamental responsibility to educate students about the Prophet's life to build a society aligned with his exemplary conduct.
The conference's theme was "State Responsibilities in Educating and Training for the Positive Use of Social Media in the Light of Seerat-un-Nabi ﷺ." Prominent scholars from Pakistan, Egypt, and Malaysia participated online, highlighting the role of social media in promoting the timeless teachings of the Prophet (PBUH). Speakers at the event included Dr. Muhammad Muslim (Khwaja Fareed College, Rahim Yar Khan), Dr. Atif Aslam Rao (University of Karachi), Prof. Dr. Muhammad Zia-ul-Haq (International Islamic University, Islamabad), Dr. Khalid Abdul Razzaq (Jamia Al Azhar, Cairo), Dr. Halim Ismail (International Islamic University Malaysia), and Khalifa Peer Qari Abdul Rehman Naqshbandi (Rahim Yar Khan), who addressed various topics related to the theme.
The conference aimed to help attendees understand how the Prophet's teachings can guide humanity in the face of social media's growing influence.

records.source_section_label: Vice Chancellors’ Conference 2025 Focuses on University Governance & Digital Transformation - KFUEIT

record_payload.name: Vice Chancellors’ Conference 2025 Focuses on University Governance & Digital Transformation - KFUEIT
record_payload.content:
Home General News Vice Chancellors’ Conference 2025 Focuses on University Governance & Digital Transformation
Vice Chancellors’ Conference 2025 Focuses on University Governance & Digital Transformation
General News News & Updates Seminars & Conferences
Punjab Higher Education Commission (PHEC) and the Higher Education Department (HED) Punjab have successfully hosted the Vice Chancellors’ Conference 2025, which aims to usher in transformative reforms in university governance, financial sustainability, quality assurance, and curriculum modernization. Held at Faletti’s Hotel, Lahore, the conference brought together 32 Vice Chancellors from public sector universities, business leaders, members of the VC Search Committee, and senior officials from HED and PHEC. The closing ceremony was graced by the Minister for Higher Education, Rana Sikandar Hayat, who was the chief guest.
Vice Chancellor Prof. Ir. Dr. Amir Azam Khan played an active role in the conference, particularly as a part of the team that presented on the theme "Governance in Higher Education Institutions (HEIs)." His insights emphasized the need for transparent decision-making processes, strengthened institutional autonomy, and digital governance frameworks to enhance the efficiency of universities in Punjab. He highlighted the importance of modern governance practices in aligning higher education institutions with global standards.
Minister for Education, Rana Sikandar Hayat, highlighted the significant reforms introduced by the current government in the education sector.
During the closing ceremony, Dr. Farrukh Naveed, Secretary HED/Chairperson PHEC, presented the conference’s key recommendations for transforming higher education in Punjab. These included implementing Institutional Performance Evaluations (IPE) and third-party audits for universities, linking performance-based funding to university rankings and achievements, and driving digital transformation of university processes to improve efficiency. Additionally, he suggested delegating statutory appointments to university syndicates or senates and restructuring the Registrar’s office to enhance administrative effectiveness.

records.source_section_label: KFUEIT Observes International Day of the Girl Child with Special Seminar - KFUEIT

record_payload.name: KFUEIT Observes International Day of the Girl Child with Special Seminar - KFUEIT
record_payload.content:
Home Events KFUEIT Observes International Day of the Girl Child with Special Seminar
KFUEIT Observes International Day of the Girl Child with Special Seminar
Events General News Health and Safety
Rahim Yar Khan, October 11, 2024 – Khwaja Fareed University of Engineering and Information Technology (KFUEIT) organized a special seminar in observance of the International Day of the Girl Child. The event, held under the Directorate of Student Affairs, featured Dr. M. Anwar Farooq, Director of the Institute of Humanities and Arts, as the guest speaker.
In his address, Dr. Anwar Farooq highlighted key issues impacting girls around the world, focusing on three main topics: the importance of education, healthcare, and protection from violence and exploitation. “Education is the key to unlocking the potential of girls and creating a more equitable society,” said Dr. Farooq, urging students and faculty to advocate for quality education for girls everywhere. He also emphasized the need for accessible healthcare, stating, “Girls must have access to healthcare services that ensure their physical and mental well-being.” Finally, he discussed the urgent need for stronger measures to protect girls from violence and exploitation, adding, “We must create safe environments where girls can grow up without fear of harm.”
Vice Chancellor Prof. Ir. Dr. Amir Azam Khan, in his remarks, reaffirmed KFUEIT's commitment to societal progress. "At KFUEIT, we believe in nurturing an inclusive environment where every individual, regardless of gender, can achieve their full potential. Our institution stands firmly with initiatives that uplift and empower girls and women,” he stated.
The seminar aimed to raise awareness among students and faculty, inspiring them to become advocates for gender equality and the rights of girls in society.

KFUEIT Hosts Impressive Degree Show by Textile Design and Fine Arts Students
General News News & Updates Art & Project Exhibition
Rahim Yar Khan (18-11-2025 ) The Department of Textile Designing and Fine Arts at Khwaja Fareed University of Engineering and Information Technology (KFUEIT) organized an impressive exhibition showcasing the final-year projects of its students. Held under the title Degree Show, the event featured the creative work of 36 Textile Design students and 5 Fine Arts students, all developed under the supervision of Arshad Rehmani and Tayyab Raza.
The exhibition was inaugurated by Vice Chancellor Prof. Dr. Amir Azam Khan. Registrar Dr. Javed Iqbal, Director ORIC Dr. Saif-ur-Rehman, Director Legal Mian Irfan, Director Institute of Humanities Dr. Muhammad Anwar Farooq, along with a large number of faculty members and students, attended the ceremony. The artworks presented by the students remained the center of attention throughout the event.
Vice Chancellor Prof. Dr. Amir Azam Khan praised the outstanding abilities of the students and faculty, stating that the Degree Show is a testament to their exceptional talent. He emphasized that KFUEIT is not limited to engineering and technology; rather, it is equally committed to promoting arts, humanities, and fine arts.
The exhibition featured a diverse collection of traditional and modern textile designs, paintings, sketches, and sculptures crafted from various materials. The displayed artworks not only reflected the students’ creative vision but also beautifully showcased the blend of contemporary trends with cultural heritage. Each piece highlighted harmony in colors, meticulous detailing, and deep conceptual thought.

records.source_section_label: International Relations Expo 2025: Culture as Diplomacy - KFUEIT

record_payload.title: International Relations Expo 2025: Culture as Diplomacy - KFUEIT
record_payload.content:
Home General News International Relations Expo 2025: Culture as Diplomacy
International Relations Expo 2025: Culture as Diplomacy
General News News & Updates Art & Project Exhibition
On 26 November 2025 , the International Relations Society (IRS) at Khwaja Fareed University of Engineering and Information Technology (KFUEIT) successfully organized the International Relations Expo 2025 under the theme “Culture as Diplomacy: How Traditions Shape International Relations and Cooperation.” The expo featured representations of 12 countries —China, Pakistan, Turkey, Iran, Saudi Arabia, Palestine, Uzbekistan, Qatar, the United States, Germany, Ethiopia, and Brazil—presented through immersive and interactive student-led stalls highlighting their cultural heritage, traditions, and diplomatic significance.
The event was honored by the presence of Pro Vice Chancellor Dr. Shehzad Murtaza , Director IHA Dr. Muhammad Anwar Farooq , Advisor IRS Mr. Ehsan Ali , Additional Controller Examination Mr. Yasir Arafat , along with esteemed faculty members including Dr. Mehwish Muzaffar, Mr. Haris Kabir, Mr. Fazzal Abbas, Dr. Ghulam Qasim, Ms. Misbah Wahid, Mr. Zain-ul-Abedeen Arshad, Dr. Majid Rasheed, and Dr. Mazhar Hussain . The dignitaries commended the organizers and participants for their dedication and exceptional execution.
By effectively combining academic learning with active engagement, the International Relations Expo 2025 promoted global awareness, cross-cultural understanding, and experiential learning, marking a significant achievement for the International Relations Society at KFUEIT.

records.source_section_label: First Exhibition of Semester Projects in Computer Engineering (ESPiCE) - KFUEIT

record_payload.name: First Exhibition of Semester Projects in Computer Engineering (ESPiCE) - KFUEIT
record_payload.content:
Home Happenings At KFUEIT First Exhibition of Semester Projects in Computer Engineering (ESPiCE)
First Exhibition of Semester Projects in Computer Engineering (ESPiCE)
Happenings At KFUEIT Art & Project Exhibition
department of computer engineering has organized its first espice on march 1, 2018, in civil building kfueit. we have introduced espice to gather all the departments to showcase their projects work with all the university. the espice brought together students from many departments such as computer sciences, electrical engineering and civil engineering under one umbrella to showcase their talent in the form of different projects.
we got 22 groups from bscs 1st section a and b (supervised by engr. dr. naeem maroof and co-supervised by engr. zainab saleem), 12 groups from bscs 1st section c (supervised by engr. hammad shahab), 18 groups from bs electrical engineering (supervised by engr. abdul basit and engr. muhammad kamran) and 4 groups from bs civil engineering (supervised by engr. dr. hamza ahmad madni and co-supervised by mr. hamail ayaz).
the students displayed their work with so much eagerness the whole day in the meanwhile our respected judges evaluated the students. the closing ceremony started with recitation of holy quran and followed by remarkable speeches from worth vice chancellor engr. prof. dr. athar mahboob and head of department computer engineering, engr. ahmad sohaib. in the last positions were announced for the first 3 best projects.
1st position -> project title "cricket mania" supervised by engr. dr. naeem maroof and co-supervised by engr. zainab saleem
bscs 1st (b), talha asghar, muhammad junaid nawab, muhammad badar and abdul basit
2nd position -> project title "pizza ordering system using c++ and vb" supervised by engr. hammad shahab
bscs 1st (c) faizan qadeer, kiran yaqoob, kainat saleem and rakshanda qasim
3rd position -> project title "electrical installation and working cycle analyzer" supervised by engr. abdul basit
bs electrical engineering 2nd semester anusha fatima, rizwan zafar, waqas khan and nida tahir
http://cpe.kfueit.edu.pk/index.php/news-events/92-first-espice-exhibition-of-semester-projects-in-computer-engineering

records.source_section_label: KFUEIT Shines at Lahore Science Mela 2025! - KFUEIT

record_payload.name: KFUEIT Shines at Lahore Science Mela 2025! - KFUEIT
record_payload.content:
Home General News KFUEIT Shines at Lahore Science Mela 2025!
KFUEIT Shines at Lahore Science Mela 2025!
General News News & Updates Art & Project Exhibition
We are delighted to share that the students of Khwaja Fareed University of Engineering & Information Technology (KFUEIT) proudly represented the university at the Lahore Science Mela (LSM) 2025 — Pakistan’s largest and South Asia’s most vibrant citizen science festival. This mega event, organized by the Khwarizmi Science Society (KSS), attracts 100,000+ visitors and brings together schools, colleges, and universities from across the country to showcase innovation, creativity, and scientific spirit. Lahore Science Mela is a flagship initiative of Khwarizmi Science Society (KSS) — Pakistan’s oldest and most influential grassroots science awareness movement since 1996.
KFUEIT students actively presented two remarkable and impactful projects:
1. Solar-Rechargeable Electric Bike
A sustainable, eco-friendly mobility solution designed to reduce carbon emissions and promote green transportation. The innovation and practicality of this prototype captivated participants of all ages.
2. IoT-Based mergency Ventilator & Patient Health Monitoring System
A life-saving, low-cost medical technology aimed at supporting emergency healthcare. With real-time patient monitoring and automated ventilation support, this project reflects our students’ commitment to solving real-world challenges.
These exhibits received exceptional appreciation from visitors, scientists, industry representatives, and fellow exhibitors. I am immensely proud of the dedication, creativity, and hard work of our students and faculty teams for bringing such meaningful innovations to the national stage.
We extend our sincere gratitude to Khwarizmi Science Society (KSS) and Dr. Muhammad Sabieh Anwar, General Secretary KSS, for extending a warm invitation to KFUEIT and appreciating the innovative projects of our students. A special thanks to Ms. Hijab Zahra, Science Communicator at KSS, for coordinating and facilitating this opportunity.
Events like the Lahore Science Mela play a vital role in fostering a scientific culture in Pakistan, and KFUEIT remains committed to contributing meaningfully to such national initiatives.
Proud moment for KFUEIT. Proud moment for Rahim Yar Khan. Proud moment for Pakistan.
Together, let’s continue empowering our youth to innovate, create, and inspire!

records.source_section_label: Institute of Information Technology Hosts Final Year Project-II Exhibition for BS-IT Students - KFUEIT

record_payload.program_name: Institute of Information Technology Hosts Final Year Project-II Exhibition for BS-IT Students - KFUEIT
record_payload.overview_text:
Home General News Institute of Information Technology Hosts Final Year Project-II Exhibition for BS-IT Students
Institute of Information Technology Hosts Final Year Project-II Exhibition for BS-IT Students
General News News & Updates Art & Project Exhibition
The Institute of Information Technology held the Final Year Project-II Exhibition as part of the students' external project evaluation process. Eighth-semester students of BS-IT presented their projects and delivered poster presentations to attendees at the ICT Building. External evaluators conducted panel evaluations based on criteria such as design and implementation, modern tools, impact on society, professional ethics, project management and teamwork, presentation, and documentation.
Distinguished guests included Zia Ahmed, CEO of GenWizz Solutions; Muhammad Nabil, founder of Semantic Solutions; Abdul Rehman, CEO at Semantic Solutions; and Umer Shafique, CEO of Promotezz International, who served as external evaluators. Faculty members from the IT department were also in attendance, including Dr. Madiha Amjad, Mr. Aqeel Ur Rehman, Dr. Muhammad Rizwan, Mr. Syed Ali Jafar Zaidi, and Mr. Muhammad Adeen Riaz.
Dr. Kashif Munir, in-charge of the Institute of Information Technology, welcomed the guests and encouraged the students and their supervisors for their efforts. He also appreciated the efforts of the FYP coordinator and advisor to the ITEC Society of IT, Mr. Muhammad Adeen Riaz, in arranging the activity.

Autism Awareness: Early Identification for Better Outcomes
Autism can be identified early through noticeable changes in speech, eye contact, and social skills. Early identification plays a crucial role in improving understanding, raising awareness, and enabling timely intervention for better developmental outcomes.
This autism awareness initiative is organized by the Institute of Health Sciences , in collaboration with the Differently Abled Self Help Society (DASH) , with the aim of promoting inclusion, education, and support for individuals on the autism spectrum.

record_payload.name: KFUEIT Hosts Special Training Session on 'Academia, Youth, and Alternative Leadership' - KFUEIT
record_payload.content:
Home General News KFUEIT Hosts Special Training Session on 'Academia, Youth, and Alternative Leadership'
KFUEIT Hosts Special Training Session on 'Academia, Youth, and Alternative Leadership'
General News Institute of Humanities & Social Sciences News & Events
Khwaja Fareed University of Engineering and Information Technology (KFUEIT) organized a specialized training session focusing on 'Academia, Youth, and Alternative Leadership' for students in Semester 07 & 08. The training session, held for Course Representatives (CRs) and Group Representatives (GRs), aimed to enhance their leadership skills and foster a deeper understanding of the role of academia and youth in alternative leadership.
The distinguished guest speaker for the event was Director Shahid Minhas from the Sustainable Development Policy Institute (SDPI). With expertise in the field, Director Minhas shared valuable insights and perspectives on the importance of academia and youth in shaping alternative leadership narratives.
The event was honored by the presence of the esteemed Vice-Chancellor, Professor Dr. Shahzad Murtaza, who graced the occasion with his wisdom and encouragement. His presence added significance to the training session, highlighting the university's commitment to holistic education and the development of future leaders.
Dr. Anwar Farooq, Director of the Institute of Humanities and Arts, was also in attendance, further enriching the session with his presence and expertise.
The training session served as a platform for students to gain valuable knowledge and skills that go beyond the traditional academic curriculum, empowering them to become effective leaders and contributors to society.

records.source_section_label: Times Higher Education Impact Ranking - KFUEIT

record_payload.title: Times Higher Education Impact Ranking - KFUEIT
record_payload.content:
Home General News Times Higher Education Impact Ranking
Times Higher Education Impact Ranking
Times Higher Education Impact Ranking.
Khwaja Fareed University of Engineering and Information Technology continues its journey of success in the field of education and research. Khwaja Fareed University is also ahead of many engineering and national universities in the International Times Higher Education Impact Rankings after Scimago. According to the Times Higher Education Impact Ranking 2022, Khwaja Fareed University has secured the first position among the engineering universities in the category of quality education while it has been included in the list of 10th and 200th to 300th best universities in the country and worldwide. According to the Times Higher Education Impact Ranking 2022, Khwaja Fareed University has secured the first position among the engineering universities of the country and the third position among all the universities in the country in the category of Affordable and Clean Energy. Similarly, in this category, it has been included in the top 101 to 200 best universities in the world. In terms of work on climate change, Khawaja Fareed University has also been ranked first among engineering universities across the country and fourth among all other universities in the Times Higher Education Impact Rankings. Similarly, Khwaja Fareed University is ranked among the 201 to 300 best universities in the world. In the ranking of hunger eradication, Khwaja Fareed University has not only got the first position among the engineering universities but it is also at the forefront among other universities of South Punjab. It ranks sixth in the country in this category and ranks among the 201 to 300 best universities in the world. Speaking in this regard, Vice Chancellor Prof. Dr. Muhammad Suleman Tahir said that the fruits of continuous struggle for the last two years are coming to the fore. He said that prominent positions in national and international rankings are the recognition of our work. The university is being mentioned in good words not only in Pakistan but also in international educational institutions. He said that continuous improvement in quality education, research and rankings would be useful for the children studying in the university in future also. This will provide students with better opportunities in terms of research, exchange programs and jobs.

records.source_section_label: Quaid E Azam Day Celebration - KFUEIT

record_payload.title: Quaid E Azam Day Celebration - KFUEIT
record_payload.content:
Home General News Quaid E Azam Day Celebration
General News Institute of Humanities & Social Sciences News & Events
Throwback to an enlightening seminar organized by the KFUEIT CSS CLUB in Collaboration with Institute of Humanities and Arts!
In alignment with the visionary mission of our esteemed Vice Chancellor, we brought together Dr. M Anwar Farooq and Mr. Waheed Shahzad as speakers to delve into the profound topic of "Quaid E Azam Day Celebration."
Anwar Sahab highlighted the distinctive features of Quaid-e-Azam, described Quaid-e-Azam's personality, revealed his mindset, discussed his potential, and shared his expectations from the youth. Additionally, he explained how we can become good Pakistanis following Quaid-e-Azam's example and contribute to making Pakistan a true reflection of Quaid's vision.
The speakers passionately shared insights on the importance of commemorating Quaid E Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah's day, unraveling his struggles and endeavors that shaped the journey to an independent Pakistan. Relive the moments of inspiration and reflection as we celebrated the legacy of a great leader and the founding father of our beloved nation!

World Cotton Day Celebrated at KFUEIT
General News News & Updates Department of Agricultural Engineering News & Events
On 07-10-2025, The Department of Agricultural Engineering at Khwaja Fareed University of Engineering and Information Technology (KFUEIT) celebrated World Cotton Day in collaboration with WWF Pakistan and Artistic Milliners. The event highlighted the significance of “White Gold” — cotton — and emphasized its vital role in Pakistan’s agriculture and economy.
The ceremony was attended by members of the farming community, representatives of the agriculture department, and other key stakeholders. Faculty members delivered insightful presentations on sustainable cotton cultivation and shared key strategies to promote cotton farming in the region.
Head of Department Dr. Yasir Niaz, Dr. Ashraf, along with a large number of faculty members and students, actively participated in the event. The participants reaffirmed their commitment to advancing research and innovation in cotton production for a sustainable agricultural future.

General News Department of Agricultural Engineering News & Events
Green Youth Movement (GYM) Club -KFUEIT is committed to continue Monsoon plantation drive throughout the month of August. Mr. Ikhtiar Ahmed Khoso, Director Center for Entrepreneural Leadership and Incubation IBA Sukkhar planted a sapling during his visit to the university.
Faculty members, staff of Climate Smart Agriculture Accelerator Program, and GYM Club KFUEIT team actively participated. Furthermore, GYM club members also installed artificial birds nests across the university campus to raise awareness and promote the sustainability of biodiversity. We extend our gratitude to HEC for their support from the Prime Minister's Youth Program.

The Institute of Humanities and Arts is a dynamic department with a highly qualified, skilled, and motivated faculty holding PhDs and MPhils in fields such as English (Linguistics & Literature), International Relations & Pakistan Studies, Islamic Studies, Fine Arts, and Textile Design. We also provide a range of specialized courses tailored to meet the evolving needs of undergraduate and graduate students across various university departments, including Engineering, Information Technology, and Basic Sciences. The Department prioritizes the enhancement of classroom instruction by incorporating modern teaching techniques and state-of-the-art technology.
Our primary objective is the holistic development of students—personally, academically, and professionally—guided by the principles of humanism and innovation across cultural, social, political, and economic domains. Personal growth is supported by effective organizational structures and resources, while academic and professional development is rooted in well-structured courses delivered by faculty members actively engaged in research. Additionally, the Department regularly organizes research-driven seminars, talks, training sessions, and workshops to foster skill development for both faculty and students.
The Institute of Humanities and Arts aims to cultivate a profound understanding of the moral, ethical, political, and ideological forces that shape our world. We believe that a thriving society is built on qualities like altruism, civility, compassion, and generosity, and the humanities emphasize and evaluate the importance of these characteristics. Department of Agricultural Engineering
Home Faculties Faculty of Mechanical & Agriculture Engineering Department of Agricultural Engineering Introduction:
Faculty of Mechanical & Agriculture Engineering
(You may follow us on Facebook for updates) Pakistan's agricultural sector plays a vital role in the country's economy, contributing 21% to the GDP, employing 45% of the workforce, and providing livelihoods for over 67% of the population. The success of Agriculture depends on a variety of factors, including access to irrigation water, modern and affordable farm equipment and structures, and a safe environment. In addition, there is a need for alternative energy sources and post-harvest food preservation technologies to ensure food security and reduce production losses.
To address these challenges, the Department of Agricultural Engineering at Khwaja Fareed University of Engineering and Information Technology in Rahim Yar Khan has been established. The department is equipped with a large infrastructure, a talented and foreign qualified faculty, and advanced engineering software facilities. It aims to serve as a hub for academic, professional, and industry mechanization development and to foster dialogue and partnerships with the public and private sectors through outreach programs, internships, and MoUs. The department seeks to provide students with the necessary skills and knowledge to contribute to the growth and sustainability of Pakistan's agricultural sector. By investing in agricultural engineering education and research, Pakistan can enhance its agricultural productivity, boost its economy, and improve the lives of its citizens. The core objectives of the department are:
Strengthen the research and training facilities at KFUEIT in the field of water resources management, Farm Machinery and Power and farm irrigation through multidisciplinary approaches by engaging the faculty for the farmer’s solutions
Advanced Solutions of On Farm Water Management Practices bas

record_payload.content:
Home Events Workshop on:Smart Irrigation System Design
Workshop on:Smart Irrigation System Design
Events General News Department of Agricultural Engineering News & Events
The Department of Agricultural Engineering at Khawaja Fareed University of Engineering & Information Technology (KFUEIT), Rahim Yar Khan, in partnership with Green Vision, presents a comprehensive workshop on Smart Irrigation System Design.
The master trainer is Engr. Muhammad Owais, Director Green Vison. This workshop will delve into the latest advancements in drip and sprinkler systems, focusing on automated technologies and sensor-based solutions. Participants will gain hands-on knowledge on optimizing water usage, boosting agricultural productivity, and enhancing landscape aesthetics. Don't miss this opportunity to learn cutting-edge sustainable water management strategies shaping the future of agriculture!.

Faculty of Chemical, Civil & Environment Engineering
The Department of Chemical Engineering is the youngest, dynamic, and most energetic department at the KFUEIT established in spring 2018. The first batch of 50 students of BS Chemical Engineering was enrolled in October 2018 after obtaining approval from the Pakistan Engineering Council (PEC).
In contrast to several other engineering universities of the country, the department of Chemical Engineering at the KFUEIT follows Outcome Based Education (OBE) system as per the Washington Accord (WA). This fact alone ensures that our graduates will be recognized in several countries including USA, Canada, England, Australia, New Zealand, Ireland, Singapore, China, Turkey, South Africa and many more.
The department of Chemical Engineering also offers a wonderful opportunity to the students of FSc Pre-Medical back ground by offering them admission to the BS Environmental Science program as compared to other traditional natural sciences programs. The environmental science graduates are in quite high demand in industries, government regulatory bodies, international NGOs, academia, and research regionally, nationally, and internationally.
The department of Chemical Engineering has established several dedicated laboratories for training and research. In line with the Vision and the Mission of KFUEIT in general and the Department of Chemical Engineering in particular, an advanced Water and Energy Systems Engineering Laboratory (WESEL) has also been established at the Department of Chemical Engineering. WESEL does not only promote research culture but also contributes positively in finding solutions for the water related problems of the world in general and this region in particular. خواجہ فرید یونیورسٹی آف انجینئرنگ اینڈ انفارمیشن ٹیکنالوجی نے گزشتہ برس کی طرح اس بار بھی کیو ایس ورلڈ یونیورسٹی رینکنگز 2024میں نمایاں پوزیشنز اپنے نام کرلیں۔بین الاقوامی ادارے کے مطابق خواجہ فرید یونیورسٹی رواں سال جاری ہونے والی کیو ایس رینکنگ 2024 میں جنوبی ایشیا کی 202 ویں بہترین جامعہ قرار پائی ہے جب کہ عالمی درجہ بندی میں یہ یونیورسٹی دنیا کی 661 سے 688 بہترین جامعات میں شامل ہوگئی ہے۔ گزشتہ برس کے اعداد وشمار کا جائزہ لیں تو خواجہ فرید یونیورسٹی نے مذکورہ دونوں درجہ بندیوں میں نمایاں بہتری حاصل کی۔ گزشتہ برس جاری کی گئی رینکنگز میں خواجہ فرید یونیورسٹی جنوبی ایشیا کی 258ویں ،ایشیاکی 801 جب کہ دنیا بھر میں 1101 سے 1150 بہترین جامعات کی صف میں شامل تھی۔ رواں سال خواجہ فرید یونیورسٹی نے جنوبی ایشیا میں 56 درجے جب کہ بین الاقوامی سطح پر 461درجے بہتری حاصل کی۔ اس حوالے سے گفتگو کرتے ہوئے وائس چانسلر پروفیسر ڈاکٹر عامر اعظم خان نے کہا خواجہ فرید یونیورسٹی کی نمایاں پوزیشنز اس بات کی عکاس ہیں کہ یونیورسٹی معیار تعلیم اور قابل عمل تحقیق پر یقین رکھتی ہے۔ انہوں نے اس شاندار کامیابی پر خواجہ فرید یونیورسٹی کی پوری ٹیم کو مبارکباد دی۔اس حوالے سے گفتگو کرتے ہوئے ڈائریکٹر کوالٹی انہاسمنٹ سیل (کیوای سی) ڈاکٹر محمد بلال طاہر نے کہا کہ بلند معیار تعلیم خواجہ فرید یونیورسٹی کی اولین ترجیح ہے۔ اس سلسلے میں کیو ای سی قومی و بین الاقوامی سطح کی ورکشاپس اور پروگرامز کا انعقاد کرتا رہتا ہے۔ انہوں نے کہا ٹیم ورک اور مسلسل محنت کے باعث اللہ پاک نے خواجہ فرید یونیورسٹی کو نمایاں مقام عطا کیا جس پر اس کے شکر گزار ہیں۔ اس سے قبل خواجہ فرید یونیورسٹی نے ٹائمز ہائر ایجوکیشن، سائی میگو، یو آئی گرین میٹرک رینکنگز سمیت مختلف قومی وبین الاقوامی رینکنگز میں نہ صرف نمایاں پوزیشنز اپنے نام کیں بلکہ اپنی متعدد ہم عصر جامعات پر سبقت بھی حاصل کی۔

record_payload.overview_text:
Home Faculties Faculty of Natural and Applied Sciences Institute of Chemistry Introduction
Faculty of Natural and Applied Sciences
Civilization started with the invention of fire or in other words it started with the knowledge of chemistry and later its contribution in providing energy, food, shelter, clothing, medicine, engineering materials, information technology gadgets and other necessities of life evidence that human being progress goes hand in hand with the progress of chemistry. Thus, chemistry was one of the programs offered at KFUEIT in 2016 for quality education and specialization in its different core branches such as organic chemistry, inorganic chemistry, physical chemistry, analytical and applied chemistry. With highly qualified and internationally engaged faculty, well- equipped laboratories and resource rich library facilities the department of chemistry is striving to prepare students to take part into national and international challenging research problems and internships for the fine tuning of their intellectual capabilities and technical skills.
The department of chemistry at KFUEIT dreams to accomplish high distinction in teaching and research, to produce chemistry experts of international level proficiencies and to build up a world class center of excellence in teaching and research within the industrial triangle of southern Punjab.
The common purpose is to achieve the highest possible standards of scholarship, teaching and research in chemistry and allied subjects.
The program for majors is designed to enable students to develop in-depth understanding of chemical principles; a working knowledge of the properties of chemicals; a familiarity with the use of the chemistry literature; skills in basic laboratory methods, including modern instrumentation; the ability to observe, record, interpret critically, and communicate experimental results; and a facility in analytical and logical problem solving.

record_payload.overview_text:
Home Faculties Faculty of Natural and Applied Sciences Institute of Mathematics Introduction
Faculty of Natural and Applied Sciences
The Institute of Mathematics is one of the inaugurals and pioneer departments in the radius of 200 kilometers in Rahim Yar Khan, which has been established at Khwaja Fareed University of Engineering and Information Technology (KFUEIT) in September 2017. The philosophy of department is to educate mathematics in much more interactive and conceptual point of view. Investigations and conceptual learning allow one to see mathematics being created by oneself. Our aim is to provide a new generation of innovative researchers and quality teachers to the nation. Our graduates will find numerous opportunities of working at research and teaching institutes in Pakistan and abroad. We provide mathematics instruction to students in a broad range of undergraduate academic programs in Mathematics, Engineering, Information Technology, Physics, Chemistry, Business and many other disciplines. The Institute of mathematics has the privilege of having 12 faculty members consisting of one (01) Associate Professor, nine (09) Assistant Professors and two (02) Lectures graduated from esteemed national and international universities. The faculty members have vast teaching and research experience in diverse areas of the subject.
Vision of Institute of Mathematics
The vision of institute of mathematics is to become a hub of mathematics in the region that revives and nourishes the mathematics culture, and generates high caliber individuals who are pivotal to the development of regional economy.
Mission of Institute of Mathematics
The mission of the institute of mathematics is to graduate qualified cadres who are equipped with insight and research skills in the field of mathematics and provide learning opportunities of unmatched breadth and depth by providing an environment where students can learn and become competent users of mathematics and mathematical applicatio

Home Faculties Faculty of Biological, Health Sciences & Technology Department of Hotel and Restaurant Management Introduction
Faculty of Biological, Health Sciences & Technology
The Department of Hotel and Restaurant Management is offering the degree program BS Tourism and Hospitality aimed to produce a business degree of a specific kind: Travel and Tourism Management, Hospitality, Food and Beverage Service Management. The Tourism and Hospitality Program aims to produce professionals with firm academic foundation in management and operation of most sectors in the travel, tourism and hospitality industry. Graduates will be multi-skilled with transferable managerial skills within the travel and tourism hospitality industry. The curriculum also incorporates general education courses to broaden the literary horizon of the student not only in domestic tourism but in international tourism as well.
To prepare professional business leaders with diverse skills who can contribute as policymakers and young entrepreneurs. To develop new tourism and hospitality products and explore diverse tourism attractions to enhance the opportunities for tourism in the region. To bridge the gap between academia & industry that could support the development of the industry.
To prepare professionals in the field of Tourism and Hospitality who could address challenging issues of the industry. The department envisions the development of skilful human capital and professional business leaders to establish integrated tourism development in the region through research and innovation.
The Tourism & Hospitality program intends to create an intellectual, collaborative, ethical, and inclusive environment for students, faculty, industry, and community to produce innovative education, leadership, and research opportunities. Quality Enhancement Cell at Khwaja Fareed University of Engineering and Information Technology, Rahim Yar Khan, organized a thought-provoking webinar on the "Impact of Quality Assessment on Organizational Transformation in Higher Education."
This captivating webinar aimed to explore the significant role of quality assessment in driving organizational transformation within the realm of higher education.
Distinguished speakers and experts in the field shared valuable insights and innovative strategies, shedding light on how quality assessment can pave the way for positive changes and advancements in academic institutions.
